transcript: How many shows left on my queue? You have 24,601 shows remaining, James. Shall I play them? Yep, play my queue. There's only so many series you can see. Only so many seasons to be binged. Before your eyes glaze over, your ass falls asleep. And your mind starts coming unhinged. You watch Netflix, Hulu, Amazon, till you can't remember which program is on. I'm not speaking of network TV, which is wonderful, obviously. So when you tire of flat screen images of actors who aren't there Playing parts in shows made far away And finished long ago Trade the remote for the near Leave your couch and travel here And experience a different kind of show The kind that's live, the kind that's live The kind that real life people recreate A new life, a show that's live, the kind that's live, the kind that real life people recreate for you, live, a show that's live, a show where when you laugh the cast will wait for you, actual people in an actual space, watching actors who are actually there, it's something unique to that time and that place, It's something you feel, it's something you share It's life, we do it live And every single moment's unrepeatable Life, we do it live It can't be hashtagged and it isn't tweetable There is a visceral bliss You only get in a theatre seeing people do this That's the bliss for which performers strive and tonight you're our guest as we honor the best of the best who do it live good evening ladies and gentlemen and welcome to this it's the 73rd annual tony awards I'm your host, James Corden, and tonight we have a riveting, we have a riveting, excuse me, sorry, sorry, excuse me, excuse me, Brian, Brian, Brian, where, where, where are you going? I, I, I just have to go to the bathroom. Now? I mean, the show show literally just started we're in the middle of the opening number I know but I really have to go really you. Get ready, this is just the start, get ready. Live, I'm here for life, I'm quite prepared to shake your extra leg for you. Eight times a week, our actors excel at dancing in heels with aplomb Eight times a week we go straight to hell Eight times a week we go gay to the plumb Live when we revive, you can't say no to mornings this unmissable Live, you can't contrive Another opening, alt, a show more kissable We work for months in advance Cause when you see us tonight, we get just one chance That's great,, take five Take five Take five Oh thanks Sebastian, I appreciate it I think it's good I think it's good, it feels tight It feels tight, it feels good Hey everybody, everybody, attention, attention Listen, listen, just remember Next time we do it, just really try and get that very Oh wait I forgot the performers cannot take a break Cause this is life Here, you're not watching a signal beaming None of our shows are streaming They don't just start any time Here, we don't charge by a monthly billing Nobody's Broadway the chilling and no one's on Broadway Prime. We're the heirs of a grand tradition. The stage is our noble mission. It runs deep in our bones. We're much better than television. We do it with great derision Well, except Game of Thrones And Fleabag And Handmaid's Tale And Killing Eve And Castle Rock And Walking Dead And Dead to Me And Stranger Things And Better Things And Anything That Ends With Things Orange is the new Black Mirror Marvel's Marvels marvelous mrs mazel barry basket blackish billies bojack ozark dark and narcos rick and morney grace and frankie and the late late show with me right here on cbs america's most watched my god what am I doing what am I saying no wait wait TV wait television wait wait wait I didn't I didn't mean anything I just said I didn't mean a single word of that and Audra McDonald's made me say it she did she did she truth is, I love you, TV. We all love you. You pay us so much more money than the theatre. Law and order, corpses. Look, let me level with you about Broadway The price of a ticket is awfully steep You buy it last minute, it still isn't cheap A trip into Midtown's a terrible drive Gotta leave Jersey no later than five Theaters are crowded and noisy and old And maintaining Siberian levels of cold Seats are so narrow they crush like a clam And with limited legroom you're likely to crash Can't take a photo, you can't choose your phone If you don't believe me, ask Patti LuPone Ask for the ladies room, I'd recommend you the tingle before I'll see you next time. They're so narrow, they crush like a clapper With limited legroom, you're likely to crap Can't take a photo, you can't use your phone If you don't believe me, ask Patti LuPone Ask for the ladies' room, I'd recommend Either tinkle beforehand or wear a deep paint But to gaze at a stage of performers like these Or any and all of tonight's nominees To be in their presence and bask in their art Thanks for watching! like these or any and all of tonight's nominees to be in their presence and bask in their art will lighten your spirit and gladden your heart the laughs fill your belly the tears fill your eyes the hair on the back of your neck starts to rise and it's so indescribably pleasant you're in the moment and totally present you go to a show cause the show lets you know that you're here and you're now and you're real and you feel alive you feel alive you feel a sense of wonder reawaken you Alive, you come alive You come along to where the show has taken you There is the audience, here is the stage Broadway's about what's in between The energy flows and your senses engage In ways that they can't when you stare at a screen It isn't the Broadway, some grand panacea But it'll remind you it's thrilling to be alive To be alive Thank you. And the Broadway's some grand panacea But it'll remind you it's thrilling to be alive To be alive To be excited and exhilarated Alive, Broadway's alive And that is worthy to be celebrated Everyone's here, it's time to begin We're glad you showed up and glad you tuned in As for me, I'll simply say that I've been unsure all along If I'd get through this song, but I did and it's done Oh my God, it was fun It's the Tonys and we are live We are live with pride! Ladies and gentlemen, the Tony-nominated book writer of Mean Girls.
